title: In-vitro CYP inhibition pooled
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4m12","text":"This assay is crucial for understanding a compound's inhibitory profile across major drug-metabolizing enzymes, providing essential data for predicting clinical drug-drug interactions and supporting regulatory submissions in pharmaceutical development.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7e2ik","text":"An in vitro CYP inhibition assay evaluates the potential of a test compound to inhibit multiple cytochrome P450 enzymes simultaneously using a cocktail approach. This pooled assay employs a mixture of substrates specific to different CYP isoforms (CYP1A2, CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6 \u0026 CYP3A4) combined with a pooled enzyme source, such as human liver microsomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f6ofi","text":"The cocktail method offers several advantages over individual isoform testing, including:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"21o7t","text":"Reduced material consumption - Single test compound aliquot tests multiple CYP enzymes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6fakl","text":"Higher throughput screening - Multiple CYP interactions assessed simultaneously","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4uvo2","text":"Early drug development efficiency - Rapid identification of potential drug-drug interaction liabilities","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4o1jk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3nkou","text":"Always wear appropriate PPE for this protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cescn","text":"Refer to Material Safety Data Sheets for additional safety and handling information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"amrts","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The pooled CYP inhibition is performed in 96-well plate format, the compounds are incubated at 0, 0.15, 0.5, 1.5, 5, 15 & 50μM concentration in a shaking incubator at 37ºC
Clearance and half-life are calculated from the elimination rate constant derived from plotting response ratio versus time
CYPs are pooled in a ratio such that biotransformation of each probe substrate is specific for the particular CYP450
Substrates (at their Km) are also pooled within the same solution to create an enzyme-substrate stock and test compound is added to appropriate wells
The supernatants are analyzed by LC-MS/MS using generic analytical methods to measure metabolite formation.
The incubation is stopped at t = 10min by removal of the plate from the shaking incubator, followed by the addition of 200μL of ice cold methanol containing internal standard, and mixing.
Each concentration of test compound is assayed against five CYPs in the same well simultaneously.
After equilibration to 37ºC, addition of NADPH and mixing initiates substrate biotransformation
The final solvent concentration is 1.0% DMSO
The quenched samples are then centrifuged to precipitate the protein.
IC 50 (μM) of test compound against CYP1A2, CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6 & CYP3A4 are calculated by plotting 6-point inhibition curves of the metabolite formation response.
creator: Nick Lynch
